data-analyst.title: Spreadsheet and Data Fundamentals
data-analyst.description: Learn to organize, clean, and summarize data using spreadsheets. Understand core statistical concepts like mean, median, variance, and distributions.
data-analyst.challenge: Load a CSV of sales data into Excel, create a pivot table summarizing revenue by region, and produce a bar chart.

data-analyst.title: SQL for Data Analysis
data-analyst.description: Write queries to extract, filter, and aggregate data from relational databases. Learn JOINs, subqueries, and window functions for analytical tasks.
data-analyst.challenge: Using a sample e-commerce database, write a query that ranks the top 10 customers by lifetime value using a window function.

data-analyst.title: Python for Data Analysis
data-analyst.description: Use Python and Pandas to manipulate and explore datasets beyond what spreadsheets can handle. Set up a Jupyter notebook workflow for reproducible analysis.
data-analyst.challenge: Load two CSV datasets, merge them on a common key, handle missing values, and compute a grouped summary with Pandas.

data-analyst.title: Data Cleaning and Wrangling
data-analyst.description: Apply systematic techniques to detect and fix data quality issues such as duplicates, outliers, and inconsistent formats. Use regular expressions to parse and transform text fields.
data-analyst.challenge: Take a raw dataset with mixed date formats, duplicated rows, and missing fields; produce a fully cleaned version with a documented cleaning log.

data-analyst.title: Data Visualization with Python
data-analyst.description: Create clear and informative charts that communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences. Learn to choose the right chart type for each data relationship.
data-analyst.challenge: Build a multi-panel dashboard in a Jupyter notebook showing sales trends, regional breakdown, and a correlation heatmap for a sample dataset.

data-analyst.title: Tableau for Business Dashboards
data-analyst.description: Connect Tableau to data sources and build interactive dashboards that allow stakeholders to explore data without writing code. Learn to design for clarity and usability.
data-analyst.challenge: Build a Tableau dashboard with at least 3 interactive views connected to a SQL or CSV source and publish it to Tableau Public.

data-analyst.title: Statistical Analysis and Hypothesis Testing
data-analyst.description: Apply inferential statistics to draw conclusions from samples and validate business hypotheses. Understand p-values, confidence intervals, and common statistical tests.
data-analyst.challenge: Run an A/B test analysis on a provided experiment dataset: check assumptions, run the appropriate statistical test, and write a plain-English conclusion.

data-analyst.title: Business Metrics and KPI Reporting
data-analyst.description: Translate business questions into measurable KPIs and build automated reporting pipelines. Learn to communicate findings through structured reports and executive summaries.
data-analyst.challenge: Build a Python script that reads monthly sales data, computes MoM growth, churn rate, and LTV, then outputs a formatted Excel report automatically.

data-analyst.title: Advanced SQL and Data Modeling
data-analyst.description: Design dimensional data models and write advanced analytical queries against a data warehouse. Learn star schemas and how dbt is used to manage transformation logic.
data-analyst.challenge: Model a sample raw dataset into a star schema using dbt, write at least 3 fact-table queries using window functions, and add dbt tests for key columns.

data-analyst.title: End-to-End Analytics Project
data-analyst.description: Deliver a complete analytics project from raw data ingestion through cleaning, analysis, and a stakeholder-facing Tableau dashboard. Document methodology and present findings clearly.
data-analyst.challenge: Complete a full analytics project on a real dataset: ingest, clean, analyze in Python, model in SQL, and publish a Tableau dashboard with a written narrative of your findings.

ds-data-scientist.title: Python and Data Science Environment
ds-data-scientist.description: Set up a professional data science environment and gain fluency in Python and NumPy for numerical computing. Understand how Jupyter notebooks support exploratory, reproducible analysis workflows.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Create a Jupyter notebook that uses NumPy to generate a synthetic dataset, compute descriptive statistics, and plot a histogram.

ds-data-scientist.title: Exploratory Data Analysis
ds-data-scientist.description: Perform systematic EDA to understand distributions, relationships, and anomalies in data before modeling. Use visualization to surface patterns and guide analytical decisions.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Perform a full EDA on a public dataset: document at least 5 findings with supporting charts and a written interpretation for each.

ds-data-scientist.title: Statistics for Data Science
ds-data-scientist.description: Apply probability theory and statistical inference to make data-driven conclusions. Understand sampling, distributions, and hypothesis testing in an analytics context.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Given two product variants and conversion data, determine whether the difference in conversion rate is statistically significant using a z-test or chi-square test.

ds-data-scientist.title: Data Cleaning and Feature Engineering
ds-data-scientist.description: Transform raw data into analysis-ready features by applying cleaning, encoding, and scaling techniques. Understand how feature quality directly impacts analytical and reporting accuracy.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Take a raw tabular dataset with mixed types, encode categorical columns, impute missing values, and scale numeric features using Scikit-learn pipelines.

ds-data-scientist.title: Descriptive Modeling and Segmentation
ds-data-scientist.description: Use clustering and dimensionality reduction to segment data and find natural groupings for reporting and targeting. Interpret cluster outputs in business terms for non-technical audiences.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Cluster a customer dataset into 4 segments using K-Means, profile each cluster with summary statistics, and produce a labeled PCA scatter plot.

ds-data-scientist.title: Regression Analysis for Forecasting
ds-data-scientist.description: Build linear and polynomial regression models to quantify relationships and produce forecasts for business reporting. Diagnose model fit and communicate coefficients as business insights.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Build a multiple regression model predicting monthly revenue, interpret each coefficient, check residual assumptions, and present a 3-month forecast with confidence intervals.

ds-data-scientist.title: Classification for Analytical Reporting
ds-data-scientist.description: Train classification models to support analytical use cases like churn prediction and lead scoring for reporting dashboards. Evaluate models with appropriate metrics for imbalanced datasets.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Train a churn prediction classifier, evaluate with precision/recall and ROC-AUC, and produce a ranked list of at-risk customers for a simulated marketing team.

ds-data-scientist.title: Time Series Analysis
ds-data-scientist.description: Analyze time-indexed data to identify trends, seasonality, and anomalies for business reporting. Build forecasting models and visualize projections with uncertainty bands.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Decompose a monthly sales time series, fit an ARIMA model, forecast 6 months ahead, and overlay actuals vs forecast on a Matplotlib chart.

ds-data-scientist.title: Reporting Pipelines and Automation
ds-data-scientist.description: Automate recurring analysis and report generation so stakeholders receive fresh insights without manual effort. Use parameterized notebooks and templated HTML or PDF outputs.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Create a parameterized notebook that accepts a date range, computes KPIs, and renders a styled HTML report; schedule it to run daily using a task scheduler.

ds-data-scientist.title: Capstone - Analytics Reporting Project
ds-data-scientist.description: Deliver a complete data science project scoped to a business analytics use case: from EDA and modeling through automated reporting and stakeholder presentation. Demonstrate the full analytics lifecycle.
ds-data-scientist.challenge: Complete an end-to-end analytics project: collect data, run EDA, build a predictive or segmentation model, automate a report, and present findings in a 10-slide stakeholder deck.

ds-data-engineer.title: Data Engineering Fundamentals
ds-data-engineer.description: Understand the data engineering role and how data flows from source systems into a data warehouse. Learn the distinction between OLTP and OLAP systems and the layers of a modern data stack.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Draw a diagram of a data warehouse pipeline for a retail business, labeling source systems, ingestion layer, staging, and reporting layer.

ds-data-engineer.title: SQL and Dimensional Modeling
ds-data-engineer.description: Design dimensional models and write the advanced SQL needed to populate fact and dimension tables in a warehouse. Master slowly changing dimensions and surrogate key patterns.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Model a retail transaction dataset into a star schema with at least one SCD Type 2 dimension table and populate it from raw source data using SQL.

ds-data-engineer.title: Python for Data Pipelines
ds-data-engineer.description: Write Python scripts that extract data from files and APIs, transform it, and load it into a relational database. Apply software engineering practices like logging, error handling, and modular design.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Build a Python ETL script that reads from a public API, transforms the response with Pandas, and upserts records into a PostgreSQL table with logging.

ds-data-engineer.title: Cloud Data Warehouses
ds-data-engineer.description: Provision and query cloud data warehouse platforms and understand their cost and performance models. Learn to load data efficiently and manage users and schemas.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Load a multi-GB CSV dataset into BigQuery, partition the table by date, and compare query cost between a partitioned and non-partitioned scan.

ds-data-engineer.title: dbt for Data Transformation
ds-data-engineer.description: Use dbt to build modular, tested, and documented SQL transformation pipelines inside a data warehouse. Understand dbt project structure, ref() patterns, and lineage graphs.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Build a dbt project with staging, intermediate, and mart layers for an e-commerce dataset; add schema tests for nulls and uniqueness on key columns.

ds-data-engineer.title: Workflow Orchestration with Airflow
ds-data-engineer.description: Orchestrate multi-step data warehouse pipelines with Apache Airflow DAGs. Schedule, monitor, and retry tasks to ensure reliable nightly and intraday loads.

resources.label: The Complete Hands-On Intro to Airflow

ds-data-engineer.challenge: Build an Airflow DAG that extracts data from an API, transforms it with Python, loads it into a warehouse table, and triggers a dbt run - scheduled nightly.

ds-data-engineer.title: Spark for Batch Processing
ds-data-engineer.description: Use Apache Spark to process large datasets in batch for data warehouse loading at scale. Understand the Spark execution model, DataFrames API, and columnar file formats.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Use PySpark to read a multi-file dataset from cloud storage, apply transformations, and write the output as partitioned Parquet files ready for warehouse ingestion.

ds-data-engineer.title: Data Quality and Testing
ds-data-engineer.description: Implement automated data quality checks at each layer of the pipeline to catch issues before they affect reports. Use Great Expectations and dbt tests to enforce contracts on data.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Add a Great Expectations suite to a staging pipeline that validates row counts, null rates, and value ranges; configure it to block the load on failure.

ds-data-engineer.title: Incremental Loads and CDC
ds-data-engineer.description: Design efficient incremental loading patterns to keep warehouse tables fresh without reprocessing entire datasets. Understand change data capture as a source of warehouse updates.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Convert a full-refresh dbt model to an incremental model using a high-watermark timestamp, and write a test that verifies no duplicate keys exist after multiple runs.

ds-data-engineer.title: Capstone - Data Warehouse Pipeline
ds-data-engineer.description: Deliver a production-grade data warehouse pipeline that ingests raw data, applies transformations through dbt layers, enforces data quality, and serves a reporting-ready mart. Document the design and deploy with orchestration.
ds-data-engineer.challenge: Build and deploy a full warehouse pipeline: ingest raw data with Spark, transform through dbt staging and mart models, enforce quality tests, and orchestrate daily runs with Airflow.

bi-developer.title: BI Fundamentals and Data Concepts
bi-developer.description: Understand the role of BI in organizations and how data flows from operational systems to reporting tools. Learn the vocabulary of data warehousing and dimensional modeling.
bi-developer.challenge: Design a star schema on paper for a sales reporting use case with at least 3 dimensions and 2 fact metrics.

bi-developer.title: SQL for BI Reporting
bi-developer.description: Write the complex SQL queries needed to extract, aggregate, and prepare data for BI tool consumption. Master window functions, CTEs, and analytical functions.
bi-developer.challenge: Write a SQL query using window functions to produce a rolling 3-month average revenue by product category from a transactional table.

bi-developer.title: Power BI Desktop Essentials
bi-developer.description: Connect Power BI Desktop to data sources, shape data with Power Query, and build your first reports. Understand the Power BI data model and relationship engine.
bi-developer.challenge: Build a Power BI report with 4 visuals connected to a CSV data source, applying cross-filter interactions and a date slicer.

bi-developer.title: DAX Fundamentals
bi-developer.description: Write DAX measures and calculated columns to create business metrics that go beyond what raw data provides. Understand filter context and row context - the core concepts behind DAX.
bi-developer.challenge: Write DAX measures for YTD sales, MoM growth percentage, and a running total, and add them to a Power BI report page.

bi-developer.title: Advanced DAX and Data Modeling
bi-developer.description: Apply advanced DAX patterns for complex business scenarios like dynamic segmentation, pareto analysis, and many-to-many relationships. Optimize data models for large datasets.
bi-developer.challenge: Build a dynamic customer segmentation (A/B/C by revenue contribution) using advanced DAX that updates with every slicer selection.

bi-developer.title: Tableau for BI Dashboards
bi-developer.description: Build production Tableau dashboards connected to live or extracted data sources and design for self-service analytics. Learn calculated fields, LOD expressions, and set actions.
bi-developer.challenge: Build a Tableau dashboard using LOD expressions to show customer cohort retention rates, with a set action that drills into individual cohorts.

bi-developer.title: BI Publishing and Governance
bi-developer.description: Publish reports to cloud BI platforms, configure row-level security, and manage workspaces for governed self-service analytics. Understand refresh schedules and capacity management.

resources.label: Microsoft Power BI Performance Best Practices

bi-developer.challenge: Publish a Power BI report with row-level security filtering data by user department, and configure a daily scheduled refresh.

bi-developer.title: Data Storytelling and Dashboard Design
bi-developer.description: Apply data visualization best practices and design principles to produce dashboards that communicate clearly and drive action. Learn layout, typography, color, and narrative flow.
bi-developer.challenge: Redesign an existing cluttered dashboard using Gestalt principles and storytelling techniques; document before/after design decisions.

bi-developer.title: Performance Tuning and Large Data
bi-developer.description: Diagnose and fix slow Power BI reports and Tableau dashboards using profiling tools. Understand columnar storage, aggregations, and query folding to optimize at scale.
bi-developer.challenge: Profile a slow Power BI report using DAX Studio, identify the top 3 performance bottlenecks, and apply fixes that reduce query time by at least 40%.

bi-developer.title: Capstone - Enterprise BI Solution
bi-developer.description: Deliver a full enterprise BI solution: model the data, build semantic layer measures, design multi-page dashboards, configure security, and present to a mock stakeholder group.
bi-developer.challenge: Build a complete BI solution for a chosen business domain: design the data model, write DAX measures, create a 5-page Power BI report with RLS, and present it live.

ml-analyst.title: Python and Analytics Environment Setup
ml-analyst.description: Configure a Python analytics environment and learn the foundational Python skills needed for machine learning analysis. Understand package management and notebook-based workflows.
topics: Python data types and control flow
ml-analyst.challenge: Write a Python script that reads a CSV, filters rows based on a condition, computes summary stats, and writes a cleaned output file.

ml-analyst.title: Data Manipulation with Pandas
ml-analyst.description: Manipulate structured datasets using Pandas DataFrames for the kinds of data preparation tasks ML analysis requires. Understand vectorized operations and efficient data handling.
ml-analyst.challenge: Given a raw multi-file dataset, merge the files, reshape from wide to long format, handle missing values, and output a summary grouped by category and month.

ml-analyst.title: Exploratory Data Analysis and Visualization
ml-analyst.description: Systematically explore datasets to uncover distributions, correlations, and anomalies that guide feature selection and model design. Build publication-quality charts for analysis reports.
ml-analyst.challenge: Conduct a full EDA on a classification dataset: identify the top predictors visually, flag multicollinearity concerns, and document findings in a Jupyter notebook.

ml-analyst.title: Feature Engineering and Preprocessing
ml-analyst.description: Transform raw features into representations that improve ML model performance. Build reusable Scikit-learn pipelines that handle encoding, scaling, and imputation consistently.
topics: Feature selection with variance and correlation
ml-analyst.challenge: Build a Scikit-learn ColumnTransformer pipeline that encodes categoricals, imputes nulls, and scales numerics; apply it consistently to train and test splits.

ml-analyst.title: Supervised Learning Fundamentals
ml-analyst.description: Train and evaluate core supervised learning models for both regression and classification analysis tasks. Understand the bias-variance tradeoff and cross-validation as evaluation strategy.
ml-analyst.challenge: Train a logistic regression and a decision tree on a classification dataset, evaluate both with 5-fold cross-validation, and produce a comparison table of metrics.

ml-analyst.title: Ensemble Methods and Model Selection
ml-analyst.description: Apply ensemble learning techniques to improve predictive performance and use systematic hyperparameter tuning to optimize models. Compare models using robust evaluation frameworks.
ml-analyst.challenge: Train Random Forest and XGBoost classifiers on the same dataset, tune both with RandomizedSearchCV, and produce a feature importance comparison chart.

ml-analyst.title: Model Interpretability and Explainability
ml-analyst.description: Explain model predictions to business stakeholders using SHAP values, partial dependence plots, and feature importance. Translate technical ML outputs into actionable business language.
ml-analyst.challenge: Apply SHAP to explain a trained model, produce a global feature importance summary plot and 3 individual force plots, and write a plain-language explanation for each.

ml-analyst.title: Unsupervised Analysis and Clustering
ml-analyst.description: Use clustering and dimensionality reduction to find hidden structure in unlabeled data for analytical purposes. Profile clusters in business terms and visualize high-dimensional data.
ml-analyst.challenge: Perform clustering on a behavioral dataset, determine optimal K using silhouette analysis, visualize clusters with PCA, and profile each cluster with business-relevant statistics.

ml-analyst.title: Regression Analysis and Forecasting
ml-analyst.description: Apply regression models and time series methods for business forecasting and trend analysis. Validate forecast accuracy and communicate uncertainty with confidence intervals.
ml-analyst.challenge: Build a 12-month sales forecast using both ARIMA and Prophet, compare their MAPE scores on a held-out period, and visualize forecasts with confidence bands.

ml-analyst.title: Capstone - ML Analysis Project
ml-analyst.description: Deliver a complete ML analysis project from problem definition through model building, interpretation, and stakeholder reporting. Document methodology, findings, and business recommendations.
ml-analyst.challenge: Complete an end-to-end ML analysis: frame a business problem, build and tune a model, explain predictions with SHAP, and deliver a written report with actionable recommendations.

data-architect.title: Data Architecture Foundations
data-architect.description: Understand the scope of the data architect role and the layers of a modern enterprise data platform. Learn to diagram systems, evaluate patterns, and speak the language of data infrastructure.
data-architect.challenge: Draw a current-state and future-state architecture diagram for a fictional company migrating from on-prem databases to a cloud data platform.

data-architect.title: Relational Database Design and SQL
data-architect.description: Design normalized relational schemas and write the advanced SQL needed to query and transform data across complex table structures. Master indexing and query performance fundamentals.
data-architect.challenge: Design and implement a 3NF schema for an e-commerce system, add appropriate indexes, and write a EXPLAIN ANALYZE query demonstrating index usage.

data-architect.title: Dimensional Modeling and Data Warehousing
data-architect.description: Design dimensional models that balance query performance with maintainability for analytical workloads. Apply Kimball methodology to real business domains.
data-architect.challenge: Design a full dimensional model for a telecom company including subscriber, service, and billing dimensions with appropriate SCD types documented.

data-architect.title: Cloud Data Platforms - Snowflake and BigQuery
data-architect.description: Configure and administer cloud data warehouses including compute scaling, storage organization, and cost controls. Understand the architectural differences between Snowflake and BigQuery.
data-architect.challenge: Load a 10GB dataset into both Snowflake and BigQuery, configure appropriate clustering/partitioning on each, and document the cost and performance differences.

data-architect.title: Data Transformation with dbt
data-architect.description: Design dbt project structures that reflect a well-governed transformation layer, with clear staging, intermediate, and mart conventions. Apply dbt patterns for large, multi-domain projects.
data-architect.challenge: Design a multi-domain dbt project structure for a company with sales, finance, and operations data; implement the staging layer for one domain with full tests and docs.

data-architect.title: Data Governance and Metadata Management
data-architect.description: Implement data governance frameworks including data cataloging, lineage tracking, ownership assignment, and data classification. Ensure regulatory and compliance requirements are met.
data-architect.challenge: Implement a data catalog for a sample warehouse using dbt docs plus a metadata layer; classify all columns by sensitivity level and assign business owners.

data-architect.title: Data Security and Compliance
data-architect.description: Design and enforce data security controls including column-level masking, row-level access policies, and audit logging for compliance with GDPR and SOC 2. Apply least-privilege access models.
data-architect.challenge: Implement dynamic data masking on PII columns in Snowflake, configure row-level access by department, and demonstrate with a multi-role query test.

data-architect.title: Data Mesh and Modern Architecture Patterns
data-architect.description: Evaluate and design data mesh architectures that decentralize data ownership to domain teams while maintaining interoperability. Compare with centralized warehouse and data lakehouse patterns.
data-architect.challenge: Design a data mesh architecture for a 3-domain organization, document the data product contracts for one domain, and implement cross-domain sharing in Snowflake.

data-architect.title: Performance, Scalability, and Cost Optimization
data-architect.description: Diagnose and resolve performance bottlenecks in cloud data platforms using query profiling, materialization strategies, and storage optimization. Design for cost-efficient scale.
data-architect.challenge: Profile a slow reporting query, apply clustering, materialized views, and query rewrite techniques to reduce runtime by 60%, and document cost savings.

data-architect.title: Capstone - Enterprise Data Platform Design
data-architect.description: Deliver a full enterprise data platform design including architecture diagrams, dimensional models, transformation layer, governance framework, and security controls. Present to a simulated leadership team.
data-architect.challenge: Design and partially implement an enterprise data platform: produce architecture diagrams, a dimensional model, a dbt project with 2 marts, a governance catalog, and a 15-slide executive presentation.

analytics-engineer.title: Analytics Engineering Role and SQL Foundations
analytics-engineer.description: Understand where analytics engineering sits between data engineering and data analysis, and the responsibilities it owns. Build the SQL foundation required for robust data transformation work.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Write a SQL query using CTEs that joins 3 tables, computes customer lifetime value, and ranks customers into deciles.

analytics-engineer.title: dbt Core Fundamentals
analytics-engineer.description: Set up a dbt project connected to a cloud warehouse and build your first staging and mart models. Understand dbt's compile and run cycle and how ref() creates the lineage graph.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Create a dbt project with a staging layer and one mart model that joins 2 staging models; add not-null and unique tests to the mart primary key.

analytics-engineer.title: Advanced SQL for Analytics
analytics-engineer.description: Write the advanced SQL patterns used in production analytics engineering: window functions, pivoting, date spines, and complex aggregations. Apply these inside dbt models.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Build a dbt model that generates a date spine and joins it with event data to compute a daily active user metric with a 7-day rolling average.

analytics-engineer.title: dbt Testing, Documentation, and Data Contracts
analytics-engineer.description: Implement a comprehensive testing strategy across dbt model layers to enforce data quality guarantees for downstream consumers. Write dbt documentation that serves as a living data dictionary.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Add a full test suite to an existing dbt project: schema tests on all primary keys, a singular test checking a business rule, and freshness assertions on all sources.

analytics-engineer.title: Looker and LookML Fundamentals
analytics-engineer.description: Define a semantic layer in Looker using LookML views, dimensions, and measures that enable self-service analytics for business users. Understand Explores and the Looker data modeling philosophy.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Build a LookML view with at least 8 dimensions and 4 measures, join two views in an Explore, and create a 3-tile Looker dashboard.

analytics-engineer.title: Incremental Models and Performance
analytics-engineer.description: Design and implement incremental dbt models that process only new or changed data to keep warehouse costs low and tables fresh. Understand merge strategies and idempotency.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Convert 3 full-refresh dbt models to incremental using merge strategy; write tests proving no duplicate keys after 5 simulated daily runs.

analytics-engineer.title: Airflow for Analytics Orchestration
analytics-engineer.description: Orchestrate dbt project runs and surrounding data tasks with Apache Airflow DAGs. Manage dependencies between ingestion jobs, dbt runs, and downstream exports reliably.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Build an Airflow DAG that runs a data load task, then triggers a dbt run with model selection, sends a Slack alert on failure, and is scheduled daily.

analytics-engineer.title: Semantic Layer and Metric Definitions
analytics-engineer.description: Define business metrics centrally in the dbt semantic layer using MetricFlow so that every tool in the stack computes them consistently. Understand dimension and measure grain.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Define 5 company-wide metrics (revenue, CAC, LTV, churn rate, NPS proxy) using MetricFlow in a dbt project with correct grain and dimension configurations.

analytics-engineer.title: CI/CD and Analytics Engineering Best Practices
analytics-engineer.description: Apply software engineering practices to analytics code: version control, code review, automated testing in CI, and deployment pipelines. Ensure production dbt changes are safe and auditable.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs dbt test on modified models only (slim CI) on every pull request and blocks merge on test failure.

analytics-engineer.title: Capstone - Production Analytics Platform
analytics-engineer.description: Build and deploy a production analytics engineering stack: dbt project with staging and mart layers, semantic layer metrics, Looker explores and dashboard, and Airflow orchestration. Document for team handoff.
analytics-engineer.challenge: Deliver a production-ready analytics project: dbt staging and 3 mart models with tests, 5 MetricFlow metrics, a Looker dashboard, an Airflow DAG, and a CI/CD pipeline on GitHub Actions.

statistician.title: Statistical Foundations and R Environment
statistician.description: Set up an R development environment and learn the foundational statistical concepts that underpin all quantitative analysis. Understand data types, probability, and the R language syntax.
statistician.challenge: Using base R, load a dataset, compute all descriptive statistics, produce a frequency table, and render a summary report with R Markdown.

statistician.title: Probability Distributions and Sampling
statistician.description: Master the probability distributions most commonly encountered in statistical practice and understand the central limit theorem. Simulate sampling distributions to build intuition.
statistician.challenge: Simulate the central limit theorem in R for 3 different population distributions, plotting sample mean distributions at n=5, 30, and 100.

statistician.title: Inferential Statistics and Hypothesis Testing
statistician.description: Apply inferential statistics to draw conclusions from sample data and test business and scientific hypotheses rigorously. Control Type I and Type II errors with appropriate test selection.
statistician.challenge: Given a clinical trial dataset, select the appropriate test, verify assumptions, run the test in R, compute effect size (Cohen d), and write a formal conclusion.

statistician.title: Regression Analysis
statistician.description: Build and interpret simple and multiple regression models, checking all assumptions rigorously. Apply transformations and diagnostics to handle violations.
statistician.challenge: Fit a multiple regression model in R, run all 4 LINE assumption checks with plots, identify and handle two influential observations, and interpret coefficients with 95% CIs.

statistician.title: Experimental Design and A/B Testing
statistician.description: Design statistically valid experiments and analyze results with appropriate tests. Compute required sample sizes and manage multiple comparison problems.
statistician.challenge: Design an A/B test for a website conversion experiment: compute required sample size for 80% power at alpha=0.05, describe the randomization strategy, and simulate the analysis.

statistician.title: Bayesian Statistics
statistician.description: Apply Bayesian reasoning to statistical inference, expressing uncertainty through posterior distributions rather than binary reject/fail-to-reject decisions. Use MCMC sampling for complex models.
statistician.challenge: Implement a Bayesian A/B test in PyMC comparing two conversion rates, plot the posterior distributions, and compute the probability that variant B is better than A.

statistician.title: Multivariate Statistics
statistician.description: Apply multivariate methods to datasets with many variables, reducing dimensionality and uncovering latent structure. Use PCA, factor analysis, and MANOVA for statistical analysis.
statistician.challenge: Apply PCA to a high-dimensional dataset in R, determine the number of components to retain using a scree plot and Kaiser rule, and interpret the first 3 principal components.

statistician.title: Time Series and Forecasting
statistician.description: Analyze time-indexed data using classical and modern methods, accounting for trend, seasonality, and autocorrelation. Produce forecasts with formal uncertainty quantification.
statistician.challenge: Fit competing ARIMA and ETS models to a quarterly economic time series in R, compare AIC and out-of-sample MASE, and produce a 4-quarter forecast with 80% and 95% prediction intervals.

statistician.title: Survival Analysis and Specialized Methods
statistician.description: Apply survival analysis to time-to-event data and extend statistical skills to generalized linear models for non-normal outcomes. Handle censored data correctly.
statistician.challenge: Analyze a customer churn dataset as a survival problem: fit Kaplan-Meier curves by segment, test for differences with a log-rank test, and fit a Cox model with 3 covariates.

statistician.title: Capstone - Statistical Consulting Project
statistician.description: Deliver a complete statistical analysis project mimicking a consulting engagement: define the research question, select methods, conduct analysis, validate assumptions, and present findings in a formal statistical report.
statistician.challenge: Complete a statistical consulting-style project: analyze a real dataset, apply at least 3 statistical methods, validate all assumptions, produce a 10-page formal statistical report, and present findings to a non-technical audience.
